rulings.Case Title: HOWARD I. MAIBACH VS. THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA, A
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Determination Of Amount Of Award Of Attorney?S Fees And Costs; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, April 6, 2026, Line 5, PETITIONER HOWARD MAIBACH, MD's, Motion For Determination Of Amount Of Award Of Attorney's Fees And Costs. (Complete tentative ruling e-mailed to the parties). Petitioner Howard I. Maibach's motion for attorneys' fees and costs is granted in part. The court awards fees of $779,227.20 and costs of $25,122.03. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA).

rulings.Case Title: WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A. VS. MATTHEW R ADAMS
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vacate Notice Of Settlement Under C.C.P. 664.6 & Enter Judgment Pursuant To Stipulation
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, April 6, 2026, Line 1, PLAINTIFF WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A.'s Motion To Vacate Notice Of Settlement Under C.C.P. 664.6 & Enter Judgment Pursuant To Stipulation. The unopposed motion for entry of judgment pursuant to settlement is granted. The court does not have the proposed judgment plaintiff lodged. rulings.Case Title: BETTY SUN VS. THE IVY AT GOLDEN GATE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Stay Of Proceedings Pending Appeal Of Order Denying Petition To Compel Arbitration, By Defendants Oakmont Management Group Llc; Well Oak Tenant, Llc Dba The Ivy At Golden Gate; Welltower Trs Holdco, Llc; Welltower Op Llc; Welltower, Inc; And Ivy Living, Llc
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, April 6, 2026, Line 6, DEFENDANT OAKMONT MANAGEMENT GROUP LLC, WELLTOWER TRS HOLDCO LLC, WELLTOWER OP LLC, WELLTOWER INC, IVY LIVING LLC, WELL OAK TENANT, LLC's Motion For Stay Of Proceedings Pending Appeal Of Order Denying Petition To Compel Arbitration, By Defendants Oakmont Management Group LLC; Well Oak Tenant, LLC Dba The Ivy At Golden Gate; Welltower Trs Holdco, LLC; Welltower Op LLC; Welltower, Inc; And Ivy Living, LLC. Defendants' (OAKMONT MANAGEMENT GROUP LLC, WELLTOWER TRS HOLDCO LLC, WELLTOWER OP LLC, WELLTOWER INC, IVY LIVING LLC, WELL OAK TENANT, LLC DBA THE IVY AT GOLDEN GATE) motion for stay of proceedings pending appeal is DENIED. After the passage of Code of Civil Procedure, section 1294(a), a perfected appeal of an order denying a motion to compel arbitration does not automatically stay proceedings in the trial court. Rather, the decision to stay the trial court proceedings is left to the court's discretion. Factors for a discretionary stay are "(1) whether the stay applicant has made a strong showing he is likely to succeed on the merits; (2) whether the applicant will be irreparably injured absent a stay; (3) whether issuance of a stay will substantially injure the other parties interested in the proceeding; and (4) where the public interest lies." (Nken v. Holder (2009) 556 U.S. 418, 434). Here, defendants' moving papers do not meaningfully discuss any of these factors. Defendants do not show a likelihood of prevailing on appeal or irreparable harm. Moreover, a stay would substantially prejudice plaintiff, who is elderly and infirm and has already obtained a trial preference order. Lastly, defendants do not demonstrate that public policy factors weigh in favor of granting a stay under the circumstances of this case. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA).
